metadata.name: Server Security Audit & Monitoring
metadata.description: Comprehensive 8-stage security audit workflow with user approval gates. Analyzes system health, running processes, security threats (brute-force, intrusion attempts), Docker containers, disk usage, vulnerabilities, and web application logs. Generates detailed reports and actionable recommendations.

nodes.directive:
Ask user for server audit configuration:
1. Server hostname or IP address to audit
2. How to access server (SSH via infrastructure-tools MCP or other method)
3. Server description/purpose
4. Any specific concerns or focus areas

Provide clear explanation that audit will be READ-ONLY (no changes unless explicitly approved).
nodes.completionCondition: User provided server access information. Server accessibility confirmed. Audit mode (READ-ONLY) acknowledged by user.

serverPurpose.description: What this server is used for

nodes.message:
🔍 Security Audit Started
Server: {{serverName}}
Mode: READ-ONLY Analysis
Stages: 8 total

nodes.directive:
STAGE 1: System Health & Resources (10-15 min)

Execute these checks on {{serverName}}:
1. CPU usage: top, load average, idle percentage
2. RAM usage: free -h, swap status, available memory
3. Disk space: df -h, df -i (inodes)
4. Network: connection count, bandwidth
5. Uptime: system stability, last reboot

Commands to run via {{accessMethod}}:
- top -bn1 | head -20
- free -h
- df -h
- df -i
- uptime
- vmstat 1 5

Save results to SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md under Stage 1 section with findings.
Provide user with SHORT summary of critical findings only.
nodes.completionCondition: All Stage 1 checks executed. Results saved to SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md. Critical issues (if any) reported to user. User received summary.
User explicitly approved continuing to Stage 2 OR user declined (workflow can end).

nodes.directive:
STAGE 2: Running Processes & Services (10-15 min)

Execute these checks on {{serverName}}:
1. Process list: top CPU/RAM consumers
2. Docker containers: status, health checks
3. Systemd services: running services
4. Zombie processes: check for defunct processes
5. Port listeners: exposed ports

Commands:
- ps auxf --sort=-%mem | head -40
- docker ps -a --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Image}}"
- docker stats --no-stream
- systemctl list-units --type=service --state=running
- ss -tuln
- ps aux | grep defunct

Update SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md Stage 2. Provide SHORT summary focusing on security concerns.
nodes.completionCondition: Stage 2 checks complete. Results in SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md. Security concerns (exposed ports, suspicious processes) reported to user.

nodes.directive:
STAGE 3: Security - Access Logs & Failed Logins (15-20 min)

Analyze attack patterns on {{serverName}}:
1. SSH failed login attempts (brute-force detection)
2. Recent successful logins
3. Suspicious patterns in auth logs
4. Geographic distribution of attackers
5. Unusual access times

Commands:
- lastb | head -100
- last | head -50
- grep "Failed password" /var/log/auth.log | tail -100
- grep "Accepted" /var/log/auth.log | tail -50
- journalctl -u ssh -n 100 --no-pager

Update SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md Stage 3. Identify attacking IPs, coordinated attacks, reconnaissance signs.
nodes.completionCondition: Attack analysis complete. Top attacking IPs identified. Attack patterns documented. Results in file. User notified of CRITICAL findings.

nodes.directive:
STAGE 4: Network & Intrusion Detection (15-20 min)

Analyze network security on {{serverName}}:
1. Established connections from suspicious IPs
2. Firewall rules audit (UFW/iptables)
3. Open ports vs expected services
4. Reverse shells or suspicious processes
5. Rootkit indicators

Commands:
- netstat -antp | grep ESTABLISHED
- ufw status verbose
- iptables -L -n -v | head -50
- ps aux | grep -E "nc|netcat|socat|/dev/tcp"

Update SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md Stage 4. Report active attacker connections, firewall gaps, process anomalies.
nodes.completionCondition: Network analysis complete. Active attacker connections identified. Firewall status documented. No rootkits/backdoors found (or reported if found). Results saved.

activeAttackers.description: Count of established connections from attackers

nodes.directive:
STAGE 5: Docker & Container Security (15-20 min)

Analyze Docker security on {{serverName}}:
1. Container logs: errors, warnings, failures
2. Image vulnerabilities: outdated images
3. Network exposure: publicly accessible ports
4. Volume security: mount permissions
5. Resource limits: memory/CPU caps

Commands:
- docker ps -q | xargs -I {} docker logs --tail 50 {} 2>&1 | grep -iE "error|warning|fail"
- docker images --format "table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.CreatedAt}}"
- docker inspect $(docker ps -q) --format='{{.Name}}: Ports={{.NetworkSettings.Ports}}'
- docker system df -v
- docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" | xargs -I {} docker inspect {} --format='{{.Name}}: Memory={{.HostConfig.Memory}}'

Update SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md Stage 5. Report web exploits in logs, outdated images, resource limit issues.
nodes.completionCondition: Docker security audit complete. Exploit attempts logged. Outdated images identified. Network exposure documented. Resource limits checked. Results saved.

nodes.directive:
STAGE 6: Disk Usage & Cleanup Opportunities (10-15 min)

Find cleanup opportunities on {{serverName}}:
1. Large files (>100MB)
2. Old log files (>30 days)
3. Docker unused images/volumes
4. Temp file accumulation
5. Package cache

Commands:
- find / -type f -size +100M 2>/dev/null | head -30
- find /var/log -type f -name "*.log" -mtime +30
- docker system df
- docker images -f "dangling=true" | wc -l
- du -sh /tmp /var/tmp /var/cache
- du -sh /var/lib/docker

Update SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md Stage 6. Calculate total reclaimable space. DO NOT delete anything (READ-ONLY mode).
nodes.completionCondition: Disk analysis complete. Reclaimable space calculated. Cleanup opportunities documented. Specific cleanup commands provided for user approval. Results saved.

reclaimableGB.description: Total GB that can be freed

nodes.directive:
STAGE 7: System Vulnerabilities Scan (10-15 min)

Check for vulnerabilities on {{serverName}}:
1. Outdated packages
2. Security updates available
3. Kernel version vs known CVEs
4. SSL/TLS certificate expiration
5. Weak file permissions (world-writable)

Commands:
- apt list --upgradable 2>/dev/null | head -50
- uname -r
- cat /etc/os-release
- find /opt /home -type f -perm -002 2>/dev/null | head -20
- docker exec traefik ls -la /letsencrypt/acme.json

Update SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md Stage 7. Report critical security updates, EOL warnings, permission issues.
nodes.completionCondition: Vulnerability scan complete. Outdated packages counted. Security updates identified. World-writable files found. SSL cert status checked. Results saved.

nodes.directive:
STAGE 8: Web Server & Application Logs (15-20 min) - FINAL STAGE

Analyze web attack patterns on {{serverName}}:
1. Traefik access logs: top IPs, request patterns
2. Nginx/Apache logs
3. Application errors
4. Suspicious patterns: SQL injection, XSS, path traversal
5. Rate limiting violations

Commands:
- docker logs traefik 2>&1 | tail -200
- docker logs traefik 2>&1 | grep -iE "error|fail|deny" | tail -50
- docker logs traefik 2>&1 | grep -E "SELECT|UNION|<script|\.\.\/|etc\/passwd|wp-admin|\.env|\.git"
- docker logs traefik 2>&1 | grep -oE '"[^"]*HTTP' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20

Update SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md Stage 8. Identify attack vectors: WordPress exploitation, credential harvesting, API reconnaissance.
nodes.completionCondition: Web log analysis complete. Attack patterns identified (.env harvesting, .git scanning, WordPress, etc). Traffic statistics analyzed. Results saved in file.

nodes.directive:
Generate comprehensive final summary in SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md:

1. Critical Issues (Priority 1 - Immediate):
   - List all critical findings from all stages
   - Provide specific commands to fix

2. High Priority Issues (Priority 2 - This Week):
   - Urgent but not critical items
   - Include disk cleanup, updates

3. Medium Priority Issues (Priority 3 - This Month):
   - Important improvements

4. Security Posture Assessment:
   - Overall rating (X/10)
   - Strengths and weaknesses
   - Active threats summary
   - Risk assessment

Create separate SERVER-ACTIONS-TODO.md with prioritized action checklist (47 items from audit).

Provide user with executive summary highlighting top 3-5 most critical findings.
nodes.completionCondition: Final summary generated in SERVER-AUDIT-PLAN.md. Action plan created in SERVER-ACTIONS-TODO.md. Executive summary presented to user. Files saved successfully.
